question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Zähle alle Zitationen, bei denen das zitierte Paper die Klasse 'Agents' hat; Teile durch Gesamtzahl aller Zitationen; Multipliziere mit 100
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte Zitationen, bei denen das zitierende Paper die Klasse 'Agents' hat
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.citing_paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte Zitationen, bei denen entweder das zitierte oder das zitierende Paper die Klasse 'Agents' hat
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p1.class_label = 'Agents' OR p2.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.cited_paper_id || '-' || c.citing_paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p1 ON c.cited_paper_id = p1.paper_id JOIN paper p2 ON c.citing_paper_id = p2.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte nur eindeutige Paper-IDs, die in Zitationen vorkommen und die Klasse 'Agents' haben
SQL: WITH all_papers_in_citations AS (SELECT cited_paper_id AS paper_id FROM cites UNION SELECT citing_paper_id FROM cites) SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM all_papers_in_citations apic JOIN paper p ON apic.paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Berechne Prozentsatz der Zitationen, bei denen das zitierte Paper 'Agents' ist, bezogen auf alle Zitationen mit Klassifikation
SQL: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label IS NOT NULL

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Zähle Zitationen pro Paper; Berechne gewichteten Prozentsatz nach Zitationshäufigkeit
SQL: WITH citation_counts AS (SELECT cited_paper_id AS paper_id, COUNT(*) AS citation_count FROM cites GROUP BY cited_paper_id) SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN cc.citation_count ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(cc.citation_count)) AS percentage FROM citation_counts cc JOIN paper p ON cc.paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte nur Papers, die sowohl zitieren als auch zitiert werden
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN p.paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT p.paper_id)) AS percentage FROM paper p WHERE p.paper_id IN (SELECT cited_paper_id FROM cites) AND p.paper_id IN (SELECT citing_paper_id FROM cites)

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Fokussiere auf Zitationen, bei denen beide Papers die Klasse 'Agents' haben
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cites)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p1 ON c.cited_paper_id = p1.paper_id JOIN paper p2 ON c.citing_paper_id = p2.paper_id WHERE p1.class_label = 'Agents' AND p2.class_label = 'Agents'

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte Anteil der 'Agents'-Papers an allen Papers, die jemals zitiert wurden
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.cited_paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.cited_paper_id)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte Anteil der 'Agents'-Papers an allen Papers, die jemals zitiert haben
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.citing_paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.citing_paper_id)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.citing_paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Berechne Prozentsatz basierend auf dem Durchschnitt beider Perspektiven (zitiert und zitierend)
SQL: WITH cited_perc AS (SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.cited_paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.cited_paper_id)) AS perc FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id), citing_perc AS (SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.citing_paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.citing_paper_id)) AS perc FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.citing_paper_id = p.paper_id) SELECT (cited_perc.perc + citing_perc.perc) / 2.0 AS percentage FROM cited_perc, citing_perc

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Berücksichtige nur Zitationen, bei denen das Paper explicit in 'content' vorkommt
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c.cited_paper_id IN (SELECT paper_id FROM content)

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Fokussiere auf Zitationen mit Papers, die in 'content' referenziert werden
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN cnt.paper_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT cnt.paper_id)) AS percentage FROM content cnt JOIN paper p ON cnt.paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Berechne gleitenden Durchschnitt über alle Zitationsbeziehungen
SQL: SELECT AVG(perc) FROM (SELECT c.cited_paper_id, c.citing_paper_id, (CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS perc FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id) t

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte nur Papers mit mindestens einer Zitation
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM paper p WHERE p.paper_id IN (SELECT cited_paper_id FROM cites)

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte hierarchische Klassifikation (falls 'Agents' Teil einer Hierarchie)
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label LIKE '%Agents%' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Berücksichtige Gewichtung nach Anzahl der Zitationen pro Paper
SQL: WITH paper_citation_count AS (SELECT cited_paper_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM cites GROUP BY cited_paper_id) SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN pcc.cnt ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(pcc.cnt)) AS percentage FROM paper_citation_count pcc JOIN paper p ON pcc.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Vergleiche mit Gesamtanzahl aller Papers in der Datenbank
SQL: SELECT (cited_agents.total * 100.0 / paper_total.total) AS percentage FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'Agents') cited_agents, (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM paper) paper_total

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Betrachte zeitliche Entwicklung (falls Jahr vorhanden - hier nicht, daher Platzhalter)
SQL: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id -- GROUP BY year (falls verfügbar)

question: Among all the citation, what is the percentage of paper ID under the Agents classification?
evidence: Kombiniere alle Perspektiven in einer einzigen Abfrage
SQL: WITH stats AS (SELECT 'cited_papers' AS perspective, (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.cited_paper_id END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT c.cited_paper_id), 0)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id UNION ALL SELECT 'citing_papers' AS perspective, (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN c.citing_paper_id END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT c.citing_paper_id), 0)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.citing_paper_id = p.paper_id UNION ALL SELECT 'all_citations' AS perspective, (COUNT(CASE WHEN p.class_label = 'Agents' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM cites c JOIN paper p ON c.cited_paper_id = p.paper_id) SELECT AVG(percentage) AS average_percentage FROM stats WHERE percentage IS NOT NULL

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: First identify papers with class_label 'AI' from paper table; Then count distinct word_cited_id from content table for those papers; This gives total unique words cited in AI papers
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS total_words_cited FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI'

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Include all words from AI papers, counting multiple occurrences; Use COUNT(*) instead of DISTINCT to get total citations count
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_word_occurrences FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI'

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Consider words that are cited in AI papers, but only count each word once per paper; This counts unique word-paper combinations
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS unique_word_paper_combinations FROM (SELECT DISTINCT c.word_cited_id, c.paper_id FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI') AS unique_combinations

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Compare with total words cited in all papers to understand proportion; Use subquery to get both totals
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS ai_words, (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT word_cited_id) FROM content) AS total_words, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT word_cited_id) FROM content), 2) AS percentage FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI'

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Focus only on words that appear in citation relationships; Join with cites table to ensure words are from papers that are part of citation network
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS words_in_cited_ai_papers FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id JOIN cites ON c.paper_id = cites.cited_paper_id OR c.paper_id = cites.citing_paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI'

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Include word frequency information; Show top 10 words in AI papers with their counts
SQL: SELECT c.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS frequency, COUNT(DISTINCT c.paper_id) AS papers FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY c.word_cited_id ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT 10

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Calculate average words per AI paper; This gives insight into vocabulary richness
SQL: SELECT AVG(words_per_paper) AS avg_words_per_ai_paper FROM (SELECT c.paper_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS words_per_paper FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY c.paper_id) AS paper_words

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Find words that are unique to AI papers (don't appear in other classes); Use LEFT JOIN with non-AI papers
SQL: WITH ai_words AS (SELECT DISTINCT word_cited_id FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI'), non_ai_words AS (SELECT DISTINCT word_cited_id FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label != 'AI') SELECT COUNT(*) AS unique_ai_words FROM ai_words WHERE word_cited_id NOT IN (SELECT word_cited_id FROM non_ai_words)

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Consider temporal distribution if paper_id contains chronological information; Analyze word usage over time
SQL: SELECT SUBSTR(p.paper_id, 1, 4) AS year_prefix, COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS unique_words FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY SUBSTR(p.paper_id, 1, 4) ORDER BY year_prefix

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Focus on words that are cited most frequently in AI papers; Show word popularity ranking
SQL: SELECT c.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS citation_frequency, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS popularity_rank FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY c.word_cited_id ORDER BY citation_frequency DESC LIMIT 20

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Include statistical measures: mean, median, mode of word frequencies in AI papers
SQL: WITH word_frequencies AS (SELECT c.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS freq FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY c.word_cited_id) SELECT AVG(freq) AS mean_frequency, MIN(freq) AS min_frequency, MAX(freq) AS max_frequency, COUNT(*) AS total_unique_words, SUM(freq) AS total_occurrences FROM word_frequencies

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Compare AI papers with non-AI papers; Show vocabulary overlap analysis
SQL: WITH ai_words AS (SELECT DISTINCT word_cited_id FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI'), non_ai_words AS (SELECT DISTINCT word_cited_id FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label != 'AI') SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_words) AS ai_vocabulary_size, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM non_ai_words) AS non_ai_vocabulary_size, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_words WHERE word_cited_id IN (SELECT word_cited_id FROM non_ai_words)) AS common_words

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Include only words that appear in at least X different AI papers; Filter for significance
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS significant_words FROM (SELECT c.word_cited_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.paper_id) AS paper_count FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY c.word_cited_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.paper_id) >= 3) AS significant_words

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Analyze word length distribution in AI papers; Check if AI uses longer/shorter words
SQL: SELECT CASE WHEN LENGTH(c.word_cited_id) <= 3 THEN 'short (1-3)' WHEN LENGTH(c.word_cited_id) <= 6 THEN 'medium (4-6)' WHEN LENGTH(c.word_cited_id) <= 10 THEN 'long (7-10)' ELSE 'very long (11+)' END AS word_length_category, COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS unique_words, COUNT(*) AS total_occurrences FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY word_length_category ORDER BY MIN(LENGTH(c.word_cited_id))

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Consider only papers that have been cited at least once; More rigorous definition of 'cited'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS words_in_cited_ai_papers FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' AND c.paper_id IN (SELECT cited_paper_id FROM cites)

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Calculate word diversity score (Shannon index) for AI papers; Measure vocabulary richness
SQL: WITH word_probs AS (SELECT c.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS freq, SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER () AS total FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY c.word_cited_id) SELECT -SUM((freq * 1.0 / total) * LOG(freq * 1.0 / total)) AS shannon_diversity_index, COUNT(*) AS total_words FROM word_probs

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Break down by subcategories if class_label has hierarchical structure; Analyze words per AI subfield
SQL: SELECT p.class_label, COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS unique_words FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE p.class_label LIKE 'AI%' GROUP BY p.class_label ORDER BY unique_words DESC

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Find the most 'representative' words for AI class using TF-IDF like approach
SQL: WITH class_word_counts AS (SELECT p.class_label, c.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS word_in_class_count FROM content c JOIN paper p ON c.paper_id = p.paper_id GROUP BY p.class_label, c.word_cited_id), total_words_per_class AS (SELECT class_label, SUM(word_in_class_count) AS total_words_in_class FROM class_word_counts GROUP BY class_label), global_word_counts AS (SELECT c.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS global_count FROM content c GROUP BY c.word_cited_id) SELECT cwc.word_cited_id, cwc.word_in_class_count, ROUND(1.0 * cwc.word_in_class_count / twc.total_words_in_class, 4) AS term_frequency, ROUND(LOG(1.0 * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM paper) / COUNT(DISTINCT p2.class_label)), 4) AS inverse_document_frequency FROM class_word_counts cwc JOIN total_words_per_class twc ON cwc.class_label = twc.class_label JOIN paper p2 ON cwc.word_cited_id IN (SELECT word_cited_id FROM content JOIN paper ON content.paper_id = paper.paper_id AND paper.class_label = cwc.class_label) WHERE cwc.class_label = 'AI' GROUP BY cwc.word_cited_id, cwc.word_in_class_count, twc.total_words_in_class ORDER BY term_frequency * inverse_document_frequency DESC LIMIT 10

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Cumulative growth of vocabulary in AI papers over time; Show how new words are introduced
SQL: WITH ai_papers_ordered AS (SELECT paper_id FROM paper WHERE class_label = 'AI' ORDER BY paper_id), cumulative_vocabulary AS (SELECT p.paper_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.word_cited_id) AS cumulative_unique_words FROM ai_papers_ordered p LEFT JOIN content c ON c.paper_id <= p.paper_id AND c.paper_id IN (SELECT paper_id FROM ai_papers_ordered) GROUP BY p.paper_id) SELECT paper_id, cumulative_unique_words, cumulative_unique_words - LAG(cumulative_unique_words, 1, 0) OVER (ORDER BY paper_id) AS new_words_added FROM cumulative_vocabulary ORDER BY paper_id

question: What is the total number of word cited under that class labelled 'AI'?
evidence: Comprehensive analysis: all metrics combined into one report for AI class vocabulary
SQL: WITH ai_papers AS (SELECT paper_id FROM paper WHERE class_label = 'AI'), ai_content AS (SELECT c.* FROM content c WHERE c.paper_id IN (SELECT paper_id FROM ai_papers)), stats AS (SELECT (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT word_cited_id) FROM ai_content) AS unique_words, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_content) AS total_occurrences, (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT paper_id) FROM ai_papers) AS ai_paper_count, (SELECT AVG(words_per_paper) FROM (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT word_cited_id) AS words_per_paper FROM ai_content GROUP BY paper_id)) AS avg_words_per_paper, (SELECT MAX(word_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT word_cited_id) AS word_count FROM ai_content GROUP BY paper_id)) AS max_words_in_one_paper, (SELECT word_cited_id FROM ai_content GROUP BY word_cited_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) AS most_frequent_word, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_content GROUP BY word_cited_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) AS most_frequent_word_count) SELECT unique_words AS total_unique_words_cited, total_occurrences AS total_word_mentions, ai_paper_count AS number_of_ai_papers, ROUND(avg_words_per_paper, 2) AS average_words_per_paper, max_words_in_one_paper, most_frequent_word, most_frequent_word_count FROM stats

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Simple approach: Find all words in paper 315017, then find all other papers containing those words
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' ORDER BY c2.paper_id

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Include the actual words that are shared between papers
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label, c2.word_cited_id FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' ORDER BY c2.paper_id, c2.word_cited_id

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Count how many words each other paper shares with paper 315017
SQL: SELECT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label, COUNT(DISTINCT c2.word_cited_id) AS shared_word_count FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' GROUP BY c2.paper_id, p.class_label ORDER BY shared_word_count DESC

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Show each other paper with the list of shared words
SQL: SELECT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT c2.word_cited_id) AS shared_words FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' GROUP BY c2.paper_id, p.class_label ORDER BY c2.paper_id

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Find papers that share at least 3 words with paper 315017
SQL: SELECT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label, COUNT(DISTINCT c2.word_cited_id) AS shared_word_count FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' GROUP BY c2.paper_id, p.class_label HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c2.word_cited_id) >= 3 ORDER BY shared_word_count DESC

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Include information about how many times each word appears in the other paper
SQL: SELECT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label, c2.word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS occurrences FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' GROUP BY c2.paper_id, p.class_label, c2.word_cited_id ORDER BY c2.paper_id, occurrences DESC

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Find papers that share words but are from a different class than paper 315017
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id INNER JOIN paper p_target ON c1.paper_id = p_target.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' AND p.class_label != p_target.class_label ORDER BY c2.paper_id

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Show only papers that share the most common word from paper 315017
SQL: WITH target_word_counts AS (SELECT word_cited_id, COUNT(*) AS freq FROM content WHERE paper_id = '315017' GROUP BY word_cited_id ORDER BY freq DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT DISTINCT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label FROM target_word_counts twc INNER JOIN content c2 ON twc.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c2.paper_id != '315017' ORDER BY c2.paper_id

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Include citation relationship between sharing papers and target paper
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label, CASE WHEN c2.paper_id IN (SELECT citing_paper_id FROM cites WHERE cited_paper_id = '315017') THEN 'cites_target' WHEN c2.paper_id IN (SELECT cited_paper_id FROM cites WHERE citing_paper_id = '315017') THEN 'cited_by_target' ELSE 'no_direct_citation' END AS citation_relation FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' ORDER BY citation_relation, c2.paper_id

question: For all words cited in paper ID 315017, state the other paper ID and class label which also cited those words.
evidence: Find papers that share words and are also cited by paper 315017
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT c2.paper_id AS other_paper_id, p.class_label FROM content c1 INNER JOIN content c2 ON c1.word_cited_id = c2.word_cited_id INNER JOIN paper p ON c2.paper_id = p.paper_id INNER JOIN cites ON c2.paper_id = cites.cited_paper_id WHERE c1.paper_id = '315017' AND c2.paper_id != '315017' AND cites.citing_paper_id = '315017' ORDER BY c2.paper_id

SQL: SELECT p.class_label, COUNT(c.word_cited_id) AS word_count FROM paper p LEFT JOIN content c ON p.paper_id = c.paper_id WHERE p.paper_id = 'chakrabarti01integrating' GROUP BY p.class_label;
